sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current: green=41, yellow=41
# I haven't clicked the G shapes inside the containers!
# Let me try clicking the G shape in the green container (middle)
# G shape boundary: [[19, 42], [23, 42]] - vertical at col 42, rows 19-23

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 42}])

cur_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']}
g_cur = [n for n in cur_nodes.values() if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] != 7]
y_cur = [n for n in cur_nodes.values() if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] != 7]
print(f"After clicking G in green container: green={g_cur[0]['pixels']}, yellow={y_cur[0]['pixels']}")
print(f"Level done: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

# Check if anything changed
if not last_action_result.get('board_changed', False):
    print("Board did not change - maybe G shape does nothing or needs different conditions")
else:
    # Check what changed
    r_nodes = [n for n in cur_nodes.values() if n['color'] == 'r']
    print(f"Red pixels: {[r['boundary'] for r in r_nodes]}")
    
    # Also check if the G shape changed
    G_nodes = [n for n in cur_nodes.values() if n['color'] == 'G']
    print(f"G nodes: {[(n['pixels'], n['boundary']) for n in G_nodes]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
